summary: This is a high-quality test suite that thoroughly covers all five core functions described in the task (compute_sha256, build_file_index, compare_trees, generate_sync_plan, execute_sync_plan) plus the data types and error handling. Coverage is excellent: all four comparison categories (identical, changed, src_only, dst_only) are tested, both dry-run and execute modes are independently verified, and all three action types (COPY, UPDATE, DELETE) are confirmed to actually mutate (or not mutate) the filesystem. Design is clean — a reusable `make_temp_tree` fixture abstracts all temp directory setup, tests are grouped into focused classes by feature, setUp/tearDown handle cleanup consistently, and test names are descriptive. Rigor is strong: edge cases include empty files, binary content, empty directories, nested directory creation, and missing-path error messages. Minor gaps that prevent a perfect rigor score: no test for very large files (chunked reading path), no test for symlinks or permission errors, and the dry-run report test only checks for filename presence rather than verifying the action label (DRY-RUN vs EXECUTE) or total-action count. Overall this suite would reliably catch regressions across the full public API.
